claim: Obsidian Copilot supports both Ollama and LM Studio as local providers; Ollama needs OLLAMA_ORIGINS=app://obsidian.md* to bypass CORS, and ollama embedding models like nomic-embed-text or mxbai-embed-large enable a fully offline RAG/QA experience.
quote: Ollama has added support for local embeddings for RAG since v0.1.26! ... You can pull an embedding model such as nomic-embed-text or mxbai-embed-large ... a completely offline QA experience

claim: logancyang/obsidian-copilot is THE Copilot in Obsidian with chat-based vault search and agentic capabilities, sitting around 6.8k GitHub stars in 2026.
Smart Connections ships a built-in local embedding model that indexes the vault automatically on-device, with around 4.9k GitHub stars in 2026.
quote: Chat with your notes & see links to related content with AI embeddings. Use local models or 100+ via APIs like Claude, Gemini, ChatGPT & Llama 3

claim: Smart Connections operates local-first by design — embeddings are created locally by default and notes never leave the machine unless a cloud provider is explicitly enabled.
quote: Embeddings are created locally by default. Your notes stay on your machine.

claim: Smart Connections has 786,090 community plugin downloads as of January 2026 and ~4,400 GitHub stars, signalling broad adoption among privacy-conscious Obsidian users.
quote: 786,090 downloads as of January 2026 from the official Obsidian community plugins directory and approximately 4,400 GitHub stars

claim: In 2026 Ollama's recommended generalist tagging/summarization picks are Llama 3.3 70B (86.0 MMLU, 40GB VRAM) for top quality, Qwen 2.5 32B as best mid-range all-rounder (83.2 MMLU, 20GB VRAM), and Llama 3.2 8B as the default for moderate hardware.
quote: Llama 3.3 70B for overall quality (86.0 MMLU, needs 40GB VRAM), Qwen 2.5 Coder 32B for coding (92.7% HumanEval, 20GB VRAM), and Qwen 2.5 32B as the best mid-range all-rounder (83.2 MMLU, 20GB VRAM)

claim: On 8GB-RAM machines Mistral 7B v0.3 is widely used for email replies and meeting summaries, while Llama 3.1 8B handles summarization and light coding within 6GB VRAM.
quote: Llama 3.1 8B runs on 6GB VRAM and handles conversation, summarization, light coding, and general Q&A. Mistral 7B's v0.3 update is used for email responses and meeting summaries

claim: llama.cpp crossed 100K GitHub stars in March 2026 with 700+ contributors and 15K+ forks, hitting that mark faster than PyTorch or TensorFlow.
quote: llama.cpp reached 100K stars faster than PyTorch (~7 years) or TensorFlow (~8 years), doing so in under three years

claim: Ollama wraps llama.cpp's inference backend with a friendlier shell, and crossed 110K GitHub stars by March 2026 versus ~100K for llama.cpp.
quote: Ollama wraps llama.cpp's inference backend in a simplified shell

claim: On Apple Silicon, Ollama runs through Metal GPU acceleration with no configuration; M1/M2/M3/M4 Macs achieve 15–28 tok/s on 8B models, with M3 Pro/Max reaching 28–35 tok/s on Llama 3.1 8B thanks to unified memory eliminating PCIe transfer overhead.
quote: M1/M2/M3/M4 Macs with Metal GPU acceleration achieve 15-28 tokens/second on 8B models ... M3 Pro/Max chips with 18+ GPU cores, Metal acceleration delivers 28-35 tokens/second on Llama 3.1 8B

claim: Ollama on Apple Silicon is now built on Apple's MLX framework, giving a large speedup by leveraging unified memory across CPU, GPU and Neural Engine.
quote: Ollama on Apple silicon is now built on top of Apple's MLX framework to take advantage of its unified memory architecture

claim: whisper.cpp is the C/C++ port of OpenAI Whisper maintained under ggml-org and sits at ~49,148 stars / 5,469 forks in 2026, providing offline GPU-accelerated speech-to-text.
quote: Port of OpenAI's Whisper model in C/C++

claim: The dominant Obsidian Whisper plugin (nikdanilov) records audio in-vault and forwards transcriptions to Claude, GPT, Ollama, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint such as LM Studio for post-processing.
quote: supports Claude, GPT, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint like Ollama or LM Studio for post-processing

claim: Running whisper.cpp locally for Obsidian voice notes is feasible but accuracy trades off below 16GB RAM; the author found an 8GB M1 Air struggled with accents while a powerful machine with 16GB RAM is recommended for usable local transcription.
quote: the local Whisper model fell short in terms of accuracy and occasionally failed to pick accents ... a powerful computer with decent CPU muscle and at least 16GB of RAM

claim: Apple Intelligence Writing Tools are now partially available inside Obsidian on macOS via the Edit menu (after Electron exposed the API), requiring macOS 15.1+, an M-series Mac, and Apple Intelligence enabled.
quote: All the options under Writing Tools are greyed out even when text is selected ... Electron framework lacked native support for macOS 15.1's Writing Tools API

claim: Apple ships official Python bindings (python-apple-fm-sdk) to the on-device Foundation Models framework powering Apple Intelligence, enabling third-party tools to call the on-device LLM directly.
quote: Python bindings for access to the on-device model at the core of Apple Intelligence through the Foundation Models framework

claim: Apple's Foundation Models framework is a Swift-centric API exposing guided generation, constrained tool calling, and LoRA adapter fine-tuning on the on-device model — making it a natural backend for local Obsidian workflows on macOS/iOS.
quote: a new Swift-centric API that gives developers direct access to Apple's on-device language model, exposing guided generation, constrained tool calling, and LoRA adapter fine-tuning

claim: LM Studio in 2026 is the polished GUI competitor to Ollama, with a built-in Hugging Face model browser, side-by-side comparison, and a local OpenAI-compatible inference server reachable on http://localhost:1234/v1.
quote: LM Studio is a closed-source desktop application with a polished GUI, built-in model browser, and visual parameter controls. Its model browser connects directly to Hugging Face

claim: The privacy case for local LLMs in Obsidian is concrete — the cloud cannot see notes, the workflow runs offline, and there are no per-token costs once a model is downloaded.
quote: The cloud doesn't get access to your notes, you can use the entire system offline, and it's completely free.
